comics.title: Side Effect
comics.alt: Brace yourself--the chirp gets pretty weird.

comics.title: Messi
comics.alt: Commentators agree that this will probably be the last World Cup in which Messi faces serious competition.

comics.title: Tethys
comics.alt: In order to carry the necessary crafting supplies, they built the ships at 12:1 scale.

comics.title: Plate Flip
comics.alt: It's great for exfoliating your skin, bones, houses, cities, landscape, etc.

comics.title: Beam Pipe
comics.alt: 'If you keep trying to spray your collaborators with the beam when they're not looking, I'm turning off the ion source and NO one will get to play with the beam!' --Physics's mom

comics.title: Nostalgia Content
comics.alt: Gen-Z got a chunk of the Carboniferous, and now all their memes are about how pathetic and small today's dragonflies are.

comics.title: Planetary Science
comics.alt: The research was overseen by the Institutional Review Board, which is what I named my surfboard.

comics.title: Detector
comics.alt: No other experiment has a lower false negative rate.

comics.title: Sunbeam
comics.alt: While weather control is typically thought of as a superpower, the unconscious ability of astronomers and astrophotographers to summon clouds is more properly classified as a curse.

comics.title: Ancestral Genomes
comics.alt: SUGGESTIONS FOR FUTURE RESEARCH: No further research is needed as it is frankly none of our business.

comics.title: Time Machine Conversation
comics.alt: It's possible to do sea navigation without a compass, but you'll have to get some spoilers from the Polynesians.

comics.title: Flag Design
comics.alt: Every place has a local cryptid; more places need a local Pictish Beast, a creature in historical art that's drawn so weirdly that no one can tell what animal it's supposed to be.

comics.title: Neutrino Project
comics.alt: We definitely put the pool in a mine for shielding. It was absolutely not to hide it from the funding people.

comics.title: 182.8 Meters
comics.alt: They rounded down to 182.8 instead of rounding up to 182.9 because 182.9 might make the statement incorrect.

comics.title: Particle Census
comics.alt: Remember, your answers to the physics census are confidential; we will not be issuing Pauli exclusion principle citations.

comics.title: Speedrun
comics.alt: Usain Bolt holds the world record in the 100 meter speedrun.

comics.title: Results Age
comics.alt: Please, we need your help. Our research suggests you're the last living descendant of the person who knew how to format this config file.

comics.title: Pullback Drive
comics.alt: "How does the spring not run out almost immediately?" "We pull it back REALLY far."

comics.title: Crystal Gazing
comics.alt: 'Beyond that lies a vale of fire through which my vision cannot penetrate' is the kind of fun thing geologists, heliophysicists, and early universe cosmologists have a lot of opportunities to say.

comics.title: Aperiodic Table
comics.alt: Scientists occasionally invent alternative periodic table layouts, which is usually a sign that they don't have enough enrichment in their enclosures.
